landing.heroSubtitle: Trade USDT with real people in Cameroon using MTN Mobile Money, Orange Money, or QuataPay — every trade locked in escrow until you're paid.

steps.title: How a trade stays safe
steps.s1Body: When a trade opens, the seller's USDT is locked. Neither side can move it until the deal completes.
steps.s2Body: The buyer pays the seller with MoMo, Orange Money, or QuataPay, then submits proof in the trade room.
steps.s3Body: Once the seller confirms the money landed, escrow releases the crypto to the buyer — minus a small fee.

console.msg1: Escrow contract initialized — seller's USDT reserved.
console.msg2: Waiting for the buyer's MoMo, Orange Money, or QuataPay payment.
console.msg3: Payment confirmed — escrow released to the buyer's wallet.

trust.title: Safety in every trade
trust.f1Body: A screenshot isn't money — escrow is. Funds stay locked until the seller confirms payment.
trust.f2Body: Identity verification, transaction PINs, and 2FA protect your account and your funds.
trust.f3Body: If something goes wrong, escrow freezes and a real person reviews the evidence.
trust.f4Body: Pick the offer, price, and payment method that work for you. No order book, no surprises.

howItWorks.heroSubtitle: QuataTrade is a peer-to-peer marketplace where verified users trade directly with one another. At launch we support USDT on the TRON (TRC20) network, and escrow holds the crypto until the deal is done.
howItWorks.buyStep1Body: Browse verified sellers by price and payment method, and open a trade for the amount you want.
howItWorks.buyStep2Body: The seller's USDT is locked in escrow the moment your trade opens.
howItWorks.buyStep3Body: Pay the seller with MoMo, Orange Money, or QuataPay, then submit your reference and details.
howItWorks.buyStep4Body: When the seller confirms your payment, escrow releases the USDT to your wallet.
howItWorks.sellStep1Body: Set your rate, limits, and which payment methods you accept.
howItWorks.sellStep2Body: When a buyer opens a trade, your USDT for that amount locks automatically.
howItWorks.sellStep3Body: The buyer pays you off-platform and submits proof in the trade room.
howItWorks.sellStep4Body: Confirm the money is in YOUR account, then release. Escrow sends the crypto to the buyer minus the fee.
howItWorks.escrowBody: Escrow means the seller's crypto is held safely until they confirm your payment landed. If there's a problem, either side can open a dispute — escrow freezes and a real person reviews the evidence. A screenshot is not money; the seller's confirmation is what releases funds.
howItWorks.escrowLink: Read the full trade & escrow rules →

security.heroSubtitle: Security is built into how QuataTrade works — not bolted on. Here are the real controls, in plain language.
security.card1Body: The seller's crypto is locked until they confirm payment. No trade releases funds automatically without that confirmation.
security.card2Body: Two-factor authentication and a separate transaction PIN are required to withdraw or release funds.
security.card3Body: Most funds are held in cold storage under the operator's hardware keys. Only a small operational float is kept online, with strict caps.
security.card4Body: The website never holds spending keys. Withdrawals are signed by an isolated service with independent caps — so a compromised website cannot drain funds.
security.card5Body: Identity verification (KYC) and deterministic risk checks help keep fraudsters out. Every verification is reviewed by a person.
security.card6Body: Every sensitive action is written to an append-only, hash-chained audit log, and balances are tracked on a double-entry ledger that reconciles against the blockchain.
security.staySafeTitle: What you can do to stay safe
security.staySafe1: Turn on 2FA and set a transaction PIN in your security center.
security.staySafe2: Confirm the money is in YOUR own account before releasing a trade — a screenshot is not proof.
security.staySafe3: Only pay from an account in your own name, and never trade for someone else.
security.staySafe4: Double-check the address and network (USDT on TRON / TRC20) before withdrawing.
security.staySafe5: Access QuataTrade only through the official website and apps, and keep your devices updated.
security.staySafe6: Report suspicious activity — fake receipts, phishing, or scam offers — immediately.
security.reportBody: If you discover a vulnerability or suspect unauthorized activity on your account or the Platform, tell us as soon as possible. Please include a description of the issue, steps to reproduce it where applicable, supporting evidence, and the date and time you observed it. We appreciate responsible disclosure and investigate all legitimate reports promptly.

fees.title: Simple, transparent fees
fees.subtitle: Any applicable platform fee is shown before you confirm — so you always know exactly what you'll pay or receive. No hidden fees.
fees.promoNote: Launch promotion: 0% trading fees for both buyers and sellers. QuataTrade may introduce trading fees in the future — any change is announced in advance and published on this page before it takes effect.
fees.buyerFeeNote: Buyers pay no QuataTrade trading fee during the launch promotion.
fees.sellerFeeNote: Sellers pay no QuataTrade trading fee during the launch promotion.
fees.depositFeeNote: Flat fee per successful deposit, deducted after the deposit is confirmed. Minimum deposit 20 USDT (TRC20).
fees.withdrawalFeeNote: No platform fee — you pay only the TRON network fee, which is shown before you confirm.
fees.adFeeNote: Creating, editing, and removing advertisements is free.
fees.disputeFeeNote: Opening a dispute on an eligible trade is free.
fees.example1Body: Trade amount 100 USDT · 0% buyer fee · 0% seller fee. You receive 100 USDT once the seller confirms payment and releases escrow.
fees.example2Body: Deposit 100 USDT · 1 USDT platform deposit fee. 99 USDT is credited to your QuataTrade wallet.
fees.example3Body: Withdraw 100 USDT · 0 USDT platform fee. The TRON network fee is shown before you confirm and deducted according to current blockchain conditions.
fees.note1: Every applicable platform fee is shown before you confirm a transaction — there are no hidden fees.
fees.note2: Banks, Mobile Money operators, and blockchain networks may charge their own fees, which are separate from QuataTrade and outside our control.
fees.note3: Promotional pricing (including zero-fee trading) may change; any new fees are announced in advance and published on this page.
fees.note4: You are solely responsible for any taxes arising from your use of QuataTrade. We do not provide tax advice.

help.heroSubtitle: Browse a topic, or read the common questions below.
help.cat1Desc: Create an account, verify email, and take your first steps.
help.cat2Desc: Why we verify, tiers and limits, and what documents to submit.
help.cat3Desc: Open a trade, pay a seller, and receive crypto safely.
help.cat4Desc: Create offers, get paid, and release escrow.
help.cat5Desc: Using MTN MoMo, Orange Money, and QuataPay.
help.cat6Desc: Deposit, withdraw, transfer, and network fees.
help.cat7Desc: What to do if a trade goes wrong, and how reviews work.
help.cat8Desc: 2FA, PIN, and keeping your account safe.
help.noteBody: ~30 short articles across these categories for launch. The questions below are a starting set.
help.faq1Q: What is escrow and why is it safe?
help.faq1A: Escrow locks the seller's crypto until they confirm your payment. Neither side can move the funds until the trade completes or a dispute is resolved — so a buyer can't take crypto without paying, and a seller can't take payment without releasing crypto.
help.faq2Q: Which crypto and network does QuataTrade support?
help.faq2A: At launch, USDT on the TRON network (TRC20). Always send and withdraw using this network — other networks can cause permanent loss.
help.faq3Q: How long do I have to pay for a trade?
help.faq3A: When you open a trade, a payment timer starts. Pay the seller and submit proof before it runs out, or the trade auto-cancels and the crypto returns to the seller.
help.faq4Q: The seller isn't releasing my crypto. What do I do?
help.faq4A: First, make sure you submitted your payment reference and details. If the seller still hasn't confirmed, open a dispute from the trade room — escrow freezes and a person reviews the evidence.
help.faq5A: No. Trading fees are published on the Fees page (0.3%–0.5% per method) and shown before you open a trade. Withdrawals pay only the blockchain network fee, shown before you confirm.
help.faqEmpty: Answers are on the way — check back soon.

contact.title: We’re here to help
contact.subtitle: For a problem with a specific trade, open a dispute from the trade room. For anything else, reach us here.
contact.emailNote: Best for account and general help.
contact.generalNote: Questions about QuataTrade, partnerships, or media.
contact.fraudWarning: Never share your password, PIN, or 2FA codes — QuataTrade staff will never ask for them.
contact.toWireBody: an in-app support ticket form (the platform has a notifications/ticket surface to connect). Until then this page routes users to the support email above.
contact.formSubtitle: We'll reply to your email. Never share your password, PIN, or wallet keys.
contact.successBody: Thanks — we'll reply to your email soon.

about.heroTitle: Crypto to cash, built for Central Africa
about.intro1: QuataTrade is the safest way in Central Africa to turn crypto into cash and cash into crypto — person to person, in the payment apps you already use, with every trade protected by escrow until you're paid.
about.intro2: We built QuataTrade because sending and receiving crypto shouldn't mean trusting a stranger blindly. Escrow, verified identities, and human dispute review make peer-to-peer trading feel safe — without a bank in the middle and without a centralized exchange holding your money.
about.intro3: QuataTrade is a product of Quata Digital Enterprise — the company building Africa's connected digital ecosystem, alongside QuataPay and its sister products. Learn more at
about.supplyBody: company mission, founding story, team or leadership details, and any milestones you want on this page (kept lean is fine).
about.value1Body: Escrow on every trade, and funds held safely until the deal is done.
about.value2Body: You trade with real people, at your rate, in your payment method.
about.value3Body: A clean, fast, modern experience made for how people actually pay here.
about.legalPrefix: Company details and legal identity are on our

adminKyc.alertInfo: {count, plural, one {# document} other {# documents}} submitted. Open each in the secure viewer before deciding. Approving raises the user to tier {tier}.
adminKyc.documentsError: Couldn't load documents — they may have expired or been purged. Reopen to retry.
adminKyc.notesHint: Shown to the user on reject/resubmit.

adminHeldDeposits.title: Held deposits
adminHeldDeposits.subtitle: Deposits parked by AML screening or by the amount policy. Nothing is credited until an admin decides.
adminHeldDeposits.readOnlyNotice: Read-only: releasing or rejecting a held deposit is restricted to super admins and compliance admins.
adminHeldDeposits.emptyDescription: Nothing is waiting on a compliance decision.
adminHeldDeposits.releaseConsequence: Releasing credits the full amount to the user's balance despite the hold. The funds become spendable and withdrawable.
adminHeldDeposits.rejectConsequence: Rejecting permanently refuses to credit this deposit. The coins are NOT returned to the sender — they stay in custody and only a manual ledger adjustment can move them.
adminHeldDeposits.reasonHint: At least 10 characters. This is the audit record for the decision.
adminHeldDeposits.reasonPlaceholder: Why are you releasing or rejecting this deposit?
adminHeldDeposits.actionFailed: The decision could not be recorded

adminDisputes.title: Disputes
adminDisputes.subtitle: Freeze-protected escrows awaiting resolution. Only resolution moves funds.
adminDisputes.dialogDescTotp: This moves the frozen escrow. Choose the outcome, then confirm with your 2FA.
adminDisputes.dialogDesc: This moves the frozen escrow. Choose the outcome, then confirm.
adminDisputes.evidenceNone: Neither party has uploaded any evidence. Resolving now means deciding on the dispute reason alone.

adminTreasury.title: Treasury
adminTreasury.subtitle: Fee revenue and platform balances, straight from the ledger.
adminTreasury.externalNote: External is a contra account and may be negative — it mirrors funds that have left to user balances vs. arrived on-chain. Reconciliation compares these against the chain nightly.

adminCountries.pageSubtitle: Enable countries one at a time as support capacity grows.
adminCountries.alertTitle: Enabling a market opens sign-up and trading
adminCountries.alertBody: Enabling a country lets its residents register and trade with each other. Disabling it freezes new trades in that market. Each change is TOTP-verified and audited.
adminCountries.dialogDescEnable: Residents of this country will be able to sign up and trade.
adminCountries.dialogDescDisable: New trades in this market will be blocked. Existing users keep access to their trades.
adminCountries.errorUpdate: Could not update the market. Please try again.
adminCountries.configureDesc: Set whether this market is live and which payment rails its traders can use.
adminCountries.enabledLabel: Market enabled (users can sign up and trade)
adminCountries.railsLabel: Payment rails offered in this market
adminCountries.railsRequired: An enabled market needs at least one rail.
adminCountries.reasonPlaceholder: Why are you making this change?

adminSettings.pageSubtitle: Emergency kill switches and platform configuration.
adminSettings.alertBody: Pausing withdrawals or trades halts the relevant queues within seconds. Use during incidents; every toggle is 2FA-verified and audit-logged.
adminSettings.withdrawalsDesc: Stop all withdrawal processing and new requests.
adminSettings.tradingDesc: Stop opening new trades (in-flight trades continue).
adminSettings.configSubtitle: Runtime-tunable limits. Every change is TOTP-verified and audited.
adminSettings.paymentWindowDesc: Minutes a buyer has to pay before a trade auto-cancels.
adminSettings.depositDesc: Minimum/maximum deposit (checked on the gross received amount) and the platform deposit fee (fixed + percentage). Below-min or above-max deposits are held for manual review.
adminSettings.feesDesc: Fee in basis points per payment rail (100 bps = 1%). All rails are saved together.
adminSettings.sellerFeeDesc: Charged to the seller and added to what they escrow (100 bps = 1%). 0 disables it. Phase 2 is typically 20–50 bps (0.2–0.5%).
adminSettings.capsDesc: Limits in USDT. The dual-approval threshold is enforced live by the withdrawals database trigger.
adminSettings.capsOrderingNote: Must satisfy: auto-approve below ≤ dual-approval threshold ≤ per-transaction max ≤ daily max, and per-transaction max above zero.
adminSettings.hotWalletDesc: Operating balance and reserve targets for the hot wallet. Reserve and alert threshold feed liquidity monitoring.
adminSettings.launchLimitsDesc: Custody and velocity ceilings for the launch phase. 0 disables a limit.
adminSettings.limitsZeroNote: All amounts are in USDT. 0 = that threshold is disabled.
adminSettings.limitsEnforcementNote: 0 disables a limit. Amounts are in USDT; queue and per-day are counts. Live reject-enforcement of these ceilings is being rolled out — they are recorded and available to monitoring now.
adminSettings.readonlyNote: Fees and withdrawal caps become editable here after the money-path hardening (they touch the dual-approval DB check and per-rail fee schema). Until then they are managed by engineering.
adminSettings.wFeeDesc: Charged to the user on every withdrawal, on top of the amount sent. This is the fee the money path actually applies — keep it in step with the published fee schedule.
adminSettings.networkFeeDesc: Display only — shown to the user before they confirm. Does not change what is charged.
adminSettings.tierLimitsDesc: Enforced on every trade and withdrawal by the user's verification tier.

adminLedgerAdjustment.pageSubtitle: Manually credit or debit a user's available balance (double-entry, audited).
adminLedgerAdjustment.warnBody: This moves real money and is the only manual ledger entry. It is SUPER-admin only, requires your 2FA, and is recorded permanently. Double-check the user and amount.
adminLedgerAdjustment.noUser: No user found for that email.
adminLedgerAdjustment.lookupError: Could not look up the user.
adminLedgerAdjustment.reasonHint: At least 10 characters — recorded on the ledger entry.

adminLedgerAdjustment.overdrawWarn: This would take the balance below zero — the adjustment will be rejected.
adminLedgerAdjustment.invalid: The adjustment is invalid. Check the amount and reason.
adminLedgerAdjustment.successBody: The ledger entry has been recorded.
adminLedgerAdjustment.confirmEcho: You are about to {direction} {amount} for {email}. This moves real money and cannot be undone.
adminLedgerAdjustment.footNote: Every adjustment is double-entry, idempotent, and written to the audit log.

adminWalletConfig.pageSubtitle: Set the custodial wallet's public key for deposit derivation (key ceremony). Public key only — never a seed or private key.
adminWalletConfig.warnBody: This screen stores only an account-level public key (xpub). It never accepts a seed, recovery phrase, or private key. Activating a new key changes where every future deposit address is derived from.
adminWalletConfig.envFallbackNote: No production key is configured yet — deposit addresses derive from the development wallet. Activate your production public key below after the key ceremony.
adminWalletConfig.historyEmpty: No production keys have been activated yet.
adminWalletConfig.xpubHint: The account-level extended public key at m/44'/195'/0'. Never paste a seed or private key.
adminWalletConfig.reasonHint: At least 3 characters — recorded on the audit log.
adminWalletConfig.ackLabel: I understand this replaces the current wallet
adminWalletConfig.ackHint: Required when deposit addresses were already derived from the current key. Only tick this during the intended one-time launch swap.
adminWalletConfig.reviewInvalidXpub: Paste an account-level public key (xpub) to continue.
adminWalletConfig.confirmEcho: You are about to make this public key the active custodial wallet. Every future deposit address will derive from it. Confirm with your 2FA.
adminWalletConfig.successBody: The production public key is now active.
adminWalletConfig.invalidXpub: That is not a valid account-level public key. Check you pasted the xpub (never a private key).
adminWalletConfig.rotationBlocked: Deposit addresses already exist for the current key. Tick the acknowledgement to replace it.
adminWalletConfig.loadError: Could not load the wallet configuration.
adminWalletConfig.footNote: Only the public key is ever stored. Signing stays offline on the hardware wallet.

adminSystemHealth.pageSubtitle: Live status of core services, the event pipeline, and the operations queue.
adminSystemHealth.watchdogBody: This page reports what the app can see about itself. It cannot detect a full outage (if the app is down, this page is too) — pair it with the external uptime monitor for outage alerts.

adminTeam.pageSubtitle: Create teammates, assign roles, reset lost 2FA, and deactivate accounts.
adminTeam.warnBody: These accounts can operate the platform. Every change is super-admin only, requires your 2FA, takes effect immediately, and is written to the audit log.
adminTeam.createInvalid: Check the email, role, and password.
adminTeam.passwordHint: At least 10 characters with upper, lower, and a digit. They enable their own 2FA on first login.
adminTeam.createdBody: {email} can now log in and enable 2FA.
adminTeam.confirmCreate: Create an admin account for {email} with the {role} role. Confirm with your 2FA.
adminTeam.confirmRole: Change {email} to the {role} role. Confirm with your 2FA.
adminTeam.confirmActivate: Reactivate {email} so they can log in again. Confirm with your 2FA.
adminTeam.confirmDeactivate: Deactivate {email} — they will no longer be able to log in. Confirm with your 2FA.
adminTeam.confirmReset: Clear {email}'s two-factor so they can re-enrol on next login. Confirm with your 2FA.

adminAlerts.pageSubtitle: Ops and security alerts — reconciliation, AML hits, stuck withdrawals, kill switches.
adminAlerts.unackBody: Review and acknowledge open alerts so the team knows they are being handled.
adminAlerts.ackedBody: The alert was marked as handled.

adminWallets.pageSubtitle: Custody overview — wallets, deposit & withdrawal flows, hot-wallet balance, node sync, and cold storage.
adminWallets.loadError: Could not load the wallet overview.
adminWallets.noHotWallet: No hot-wallet address configured (WALLET_HOT_ADDRESS). Set it to monitor the on-chain balance and reserve.

adminContent.pageSubtitle: Company details, FAQ and reviews shown across the site.

adminEnquiries.pageSubtitle: Messages from the contact form. Reply by email.

myOffers.title: My offers
myOffers.subtitle: Manage your buy and sell listings — they appear on the marketplace for other traders.
myOffers.emptyBody: Publish a buy or sell offer and it shows up on the marketplace for other traders to find.
myOffers.selectPaymentMethod: Select at least one payment method
myOffers.deleteConfirm: This removes it from the marketplace. Past trades are unaffected.
myOffers.deletedBody: It no longer appears on the marketplace.
myOffers.savedBody: Your changes are live on the marketplace.

adminUserDetail.notFoundBody: This user may have been removed.

errorPage.notFoundBody: The page you’re looking for doesn’t exist or has moved.
errorPage.errorBody: An unexpected error occurred. You can try again or head back home.

paymentMethods.title: Payment methods
paymentMethods.subtitle: Where buyers send you fiat off-platform.
paymentMethods.hint: Set the number and account name buyers should send money to for each method you accept. Shown to your counterparty in the trade room.

myTrades.title: My trades
myTrades.subtitle: Your active and past trades.
myTrades.emptyBody: When you open a trade it will appear here.

adminScreening.pageSubtitle: Sanctioned / blocked wallet addresses, checked on every deposit and withdrawal.
adminScreening.alertBody: Every outbound withdrawal destination and inbound deposit source is checked against this list. Additions take effect immediately.
adminScreening.blockedToastBody: It will be rejected on deposits and withdrawals.

download.title: Get the QuataTrade app
download.subtitle: Install QuataTrade on your phone or computer for one-tap access and a clean, full-screen experience — no app store needed.
download.noStoreNote: Installs straight from your browser. Nothing to download from an app store.
download.alreadyInstalled: QuataTrade is installed on this device
download.manualHint: To install, open your browser menu and choose “Install app” or “Add to Home Screen”.
download.iosSteps: Tap the Share button in Safari, then choose “Add to Home Screen”.
download.feat1Body: Launch QuataTrade with one tap, just like a native app.
download.feat2Body: Opens instantly in its own window — no browser bars, no clutter.
download.feat3Body: A clean offline screen if your connection drops. Your funds stay safe on our servers.
download.feat4Body: Escrow, 2FA and every safeguard work exactly as they do on the web.
download.howSubtitle: It takes a few seconds — find your device below.
download.androidSteps: Tap “Install app” above, or open the ⋮ menu and choose “Install app” / “Add to Home screen”.
download.iosSteps2: Tap the Share button, scroll down, and choose “Add to Home Screen”.
download.desktopSteps: Click “Install app” above, or use the install icon in the address bar.

pwaUpdate.title: A new version of QuataTrade is available
pwaUpdate.body: Update now to get the latest improvements and security fixes. Your session stays signed in.

adminReleases.title: Release management
adminReleases.subtitle: Publish an application release, or roll one back. Every client picks it up on its next update check.
adminReleases.versionHint: Semver label shown to users, e.g. 1.4.0
adminReleases.versionCodeHint: Whole number. Must increase with every release — this is what ordering uses.
adminReleases.minSupportedHint: Clients below this are blocked until they update. Cannot exceed the build number.
adminReleases.validationHint: Enter a valid version, build number, and a minimum ≤ the build number.
adminReleases.confirmPublishBody: Version {version} for {platform} will be offered to every client on its next update check.
adminReleases.confirmRollbackBody: Clients will fall back to the previous published release on their next check. Give a reason for the audit log.
adminReleases.publishedToastBody: Clients will pick it up on their next update check.
adminReleases.rolledBackToastBody: Clients will fall back to the previous published release.
adminReleases.errorRollback: Could not roll back the release
